Hmm, but that doesn't make sense. Let me check more carefully. Actually, looking at the earlier data, ACTION7 shifts everything up by 6 rows. Maybe DOWN also moves by 6 rows?
Actually wait, looking at the initial state: character at rows 36-41. After many UP moves to reach row 12-17, that's about 24 rows up. If each UP moves 6 rows, that's 4 UP moves. But I made many more UP moves. Let me check what UP/DOWN actually does.
Actually, let me just focus on the task. I need to align with green at rows 37-40. Character is at 30-35. I need to move DOWN by about 1 row (to get to 36-41). But each DOWN seems to move 6 rows. Let me try 1 DOWN and see where it puts me.
Wait, from 18-23 to 30-35 is 12 rows for 2 DOWNs = 6 rows per DOWN. So 1 DOWN from 30-35 would put me at 36-41, which overlaps with green at 37-40. Let me try 1 DOWN.
